name: Yates v. United States
question: Are fish considered "tangible objects" for the purpose of the statute that makes it a crime to destroy or conceal tangible objects to impede a governmental investigation, even though the term is undefined and exists in a statute that largely refers to record-keeping documents?
description: A case in which the Court ruled that a “tangible item” as defined by Section 1519 of the Sarbanes-Oxley Act is one used to record or preserve information.
